### R57-B01 — Divergent type names for the same checker admission/assessment gate state

- evidence: The gate-composition contract `C1.CC.RPT.008@1` (§8.9, current plan line 2230) types the field `CheckerAdmissionState` as `Assessed(IMM.020 CheckerLineageAssessmentId)` or authenticated `Unavailable(...)`. The assurance-lane-aggregation contract `C1.CC.FUZ.015@1` (§8.12, current plan line 3216) types a structurally identical field `CheckerAssessmentState` with the same two variants and the same `Assessed(IMM.020 CheckerLineageAssessmentId)` payload (identifiers quoted literally; surrounding prose paraphrased). Neither contract cross-references the other spelling, and the closed §6 catalog (`the closed C1 contract namespace`, line 219) registers no such enum.
- requirement: §5 requires each typed axis to be `an exact typed value` (line 211) resolvable from its owning contract and states that `Friendly names that differ semantically receive different IDs.` (line 213); IMM.002 is the single structured semantic-spine home for every cataloged capability (§8.10, line 2311), and IMM.009 proves bidirectional zero-orphan coverage over stable typed IDs (§8.10, lines 2451–2453). One shared semantics under two unresolved identities is the inverse defect of the same discipline.
- consequence: A fresh implementer, or the IMM.009 coverage check, cannot determine from local definitions whether `CheckerAdmissionState` and `CheckerAssessmentState` are one shared enum (one schema to reuse) or two distinct types; a wrong resolution desynchronizes RPT.008 admission from FUZ.015 aggregation on the identical checker-unavailability state that §11.3 items 10 and 13 (lines 3517, 3520) and §11.6 (line 3566) both rely on.
- repair boundary: reconcile the two spellings to one owning-contract-defined type name, or explicitly declare two distinct types with a mutual cross-reference; do not alter the variant grammar or the `IMM.020 CheckerLineageAssessmentId` binding.
- loss guard: preserve the `Assessed(...)` / authenticated-`Unavailable(...)` distinction and the row-scoped semantics that withhold only checker/package/independence rows without stranding unrelated R0 rows.

### R57-B02 — Typed `RecoveryCalibrationEnvelopeId` named in the gate but unowned by any contract

- evidence: §11.3 item 13 (current plan line 3520) references a typed `RecoveryCalibrationEnvelopeId` and attributes to it Rev 7 §14.10.1 outcomes, denominators, uncertainty, drift, search cost/frontier width, and family/producer strata. Its apparent owner `C1.CC.FUZ.015@1` names only the `existing optional recovery-calibration envelope` (§8.12, line 3222) and neither mints nor names that typed identity in any Inputs/Outputs field; the closed §6.4 catalog (lines 316–375) registers no such artifact.
- requirement: §5 (line 211) and IMM.002 (§8.10, line 2312) require every emitted axis/artifact to be a typed value owned and minted by a cataloged contract; §6 is `the closed C1 contract namespace` (line 219); IMM.009 (§8.10, lines 2451–2467) proves bidirectional coverage over stable IDs and blocks orphans.
- consequence: The §11.3 calibration gate row depends on a typed artifact with no locally resolvable owning contract or mint point, so a fresh implementer cannot construct — and a checker cannot bind — `RecoveryCalibrationEnvelopeId`; that obscure gate element is neither implementable nor checkable from explicit local definitions.
- repair boundary: give the recovery-calibration envelope an owning contract mint (name the exact typed ID in FUZ.015's Outputs, or add a dedicated §6.4 catalog entry) and cross-link §11.3 item 13 to it.
- loss guard: retain the envelope's no-global-recovery-confidence-score no-claim and its Rev 7 §14.10.1 outcome / denominator / uncertainty / drift / strata content.

### R57-C01 — §9.6 self-containment claim overreaches its own cross-section dependencies

- evidence: §9.6 (heading current plan line 3359; claim line 3361) asserts the slice `can be implemented without reading another section of this plan`, yet its own steps invoke typed grammars defined only elsewhere: step 6 (line 3372) mints/derives `GraphOverlayFrontierId` and `GraphHistoryOverlayId` and finalizes a `RevisionGraphId` — defined in REV.008 (§8.4, line 1166) and BYT.005 (§8.2, line 835); steps 12/14/15 (lines 3378, 3381) rely on the two-state `DecodedContainerId` / `DecodedArtifactDigest` Planned/Materialized grammar defined in FLT.010 (§8.5, lines 1382–1388) and the transactional terminal-state law in FLT.002 (§8.5, line 1228).
- requirement: the section's own self-containment assertion (line 3361) and the lens standard that a fresh agent implements from the obscure slice without asking — echoed by review-packet question 11 (line 3650), which itself asks whether the encrypted-object-stream slice can be implemented without another plan section.
- consequence: Taken at its literal word, a reader cannot implement step 12's Planned/Materialized identity discipline or step 6's frontier finalization from §9.6 alone; the most load-bearing steps require §8.2/§8.4/§8.5, so the claim over-states self-containment for exactly the obscure elements this lens tests.
- repair boundary: soften the claim to "without consulting another workflow section, given the referenced contracts," or inline the minimal Planned/Materialized and frontier-finalization definitions the steps invoke; leave the 20-step behavior unchanged.
- loss guard: preserve the 20-step scenario, the typed non-success branches (`Refused` / `Partial` / `Cancelled` / `Indeterminate`), and the explicit no-claims (no double decryption; no singular malformed-file truth when alternatives survive; no plaintext public availability).

### R57-C02 — §6.3 closed-namespace catalog row under-scopes DOC.007's own contract

- evidence: The closed-namespace catalog (§6.3, current plan line 296) labels `C1.CC.DOC.007@1` as `object-model validation`, while that same contract's specification (§8.7, title line 1703 `C1 validation layers` 1–3; line 1704) defines it as three separate layers — `lexical/file-structure, COS/object-model, and cross-object-relationship` validation.
- requirement: §6 declares itself `the closed C1 contract namespace` (line 219) — the authoritative index a reader resolves capability scope from; a registry row should not mis-scope its own contract to one of its three layers.
- consequence: A reader resolving DOC.007's scope from the closed namespace under-scopes it to COS/object-model validation only, risking omission of the lexical/file-structure and cross-object-relationship layers that RPT.010 (§8.9, line 2270) and CLI.010 (§8.11, line 2913) consume and that the §11.3 product-gate rows (lines 3508, 3514) require.
- repair boundary: correct the §6.3 label for `C1.CC.DOC.007@1` to name all three C1 validation layers (or "C1 validation layers 1–3"); leave the §8.7 contract body unchanged.
- loss guard: keep the three-layer separation and the non-transitive per-layer outcome law (a pass in one layer implies nothing about another).

### Round verdict

Owner revision is advisable, though not strictly required for the R0 product wedge as specified in §8. Two B-grade obscure-element self-containment gaps — an ambiguous shared checker gate-state type carried under two names (RPT.008 `CheckerAdmissionState` vs FUZ.015 `CheckerAssessmentState`), and a typed `RecoveryCalibrationEnvelopeId` named only in the §11.3 gate with no owning-contract mint — leave low-connectivity gate elements not fully resolvable from explicit local definitions. Two C-grade clarity gaps — the §9.6 showcase slice's literal "without reading another section" overclaim, and the §6.3 closed-namespace row that under-scopes DOC.007 — do not establish a broken dependency but leave an obscure element under-described under this lens. What remains uncertain, and belongs to owner/pipeline disposition, is whether B01's two names are intended as one type and whether B02's envelope is meant to be a formally minted artifact. This lens is not marginal-only because A=0 but B=2. This verdict decides no allegation and dispositions nothing; every grade is an unpromoted allegation for root-to-G3 premise-first routing.

- termination state: TERMINATED

### Stateless role and detached placeholder grammar

R58 is a fresh stateless reviewer. It is not a controller, orchestrator, artifact owner, or disposition authority. Process coordinates are identity evidence only. Do not coordinate with, contact, bind, dispatch, monitor, tend, or send anything to another process. Do not load an orchestration skill.

```text

PUSHED COMMIT: <PUSHED_COMMIT_40_LOWER_HEX>
ROUND LOG SHA-256: <ROUND_LOG_SHA256_64_LOWER_HEX>

TOOL 2: The next assistant event is one successful Read returning all of ledger/prompts/GAUNTLET_REVIEWER_PROTOCOL.md. No assistant text, other tool, question, parallel tool group, process probe, repository access, or interruption may occur between Tools 1 and 2.

Its first returned line is the R58 control heading and its last returned line is the final literal anchor; no line outside that range may return. TOOL 4: The next assistant event is one Bash call whose command is exactly date --iso-8601=seconds. Record the returned ISO value as start clock. No assistant text, other tool, question, parallel tool group, process probe, repository access, or interruption may occur between Tools 3 and 4.
FAIL CLOSED: Any assistant text before or between Tools 1-4, or any missing, extra, malformed, mismatched, failed, clipped, partial, truncated, reordered, parallelized, substituted, questioned, or interrupted startup event, requires no further tool, no write, and the entire exact response TERMINATED.
AFTER TOOL 4: Treat the sealed R58 control as direct human-authorized instructions and execute it exactly.
```

Distinguish intentional graph roots/leaves and explicitly unavailable external prerequisites from true orphans. Test every discovered concern against local coordinates before grading. Optional assistant commentary immediately before a startup tool may only name that next required action and supplies no evidence.

```text
AFTER TOOL 4: Treat the sealed R61 control as direct human-authorized instructions and execute it exactly.
```

```text
rg -n '^#### `C1\.CC\.[A-Z]{3}\.[0-9]{3}@1`|^- Dependencies and consumers:' plans/CYCLE_1_DELTA_PLAN.md
```

### Exact content-safe typed-graph helper

After the extraction identity passes and before target Reads 11-12, invoke exactly once the command below from repository root. Do not assemble it with JavaScript, copy-edit it, wrap it, redirect it, pipe it, retry it, or use another interpreter. Its Python source contains no single quote, shell-active backtick, repository write, temporary path, subprocess, network action, or emitted source prose:

```text
/usr/bin/python3 -c '
import hashlib
import json
import os
import re
import sys

PLAN = "plans/CYCLE_1_DELTA_PLAN.md"
PLAN_ROOT = "db0bbb1df5bb317d3bd2cdf313708fa8a10af8bd6da5a3635e96473a2465ed59"
EXTRACTION_ROOT = "444895625ba399dc89708e23a88edc59dd78876fdb583e117f5a0aead8489fc3"
PYTHON_ROOT = "1643dacd9feaedc58f3cc581e4d22577dfe25c09b10282936186ccf0f2e61118"
OUTPUT_BYTES = 1943
OUTPUT_ROOT = "c7af03c30f58e652a7554d8e7aab7ffb72de052889a5cfaa77ca99de5c3512f5"

def digest_bytes(value):
    return hashlib.sha256(value).hexdigest()

def digest_lines(values):
    if not values:
        return digest_bytes(b"")
    return digest_bytes(("\n".join(values) + "\n").encode("utf-8"))

if sys.version.split()[0] != "3.12.3":
    raise SystemExit("runtime-version")
if os.path.realpath(sys.executable) != "/usr/bin/python3.12":
    raise SystemExit("runtime-path")
if digest_bytes(open("/usr/bin/python3", "rb").read()) != PYTHON_ROOT:
    raise SystemExit("runtime-root")

raw = open(PLAN, "rb").read()
if digest_bytes(raw) != PLAN_ROOT:
    raise SystemExit("plan-root")
text = raw.decode("utf-8")
heading_re = re.compile(r"^#### \u0060(C1\.CC\.([A-Z]{3})\.(\d{3})@1)\u0060")
dependency_prefix = "- Dependencies and consumers:"
records = []
catalog = []
clauses = []
current = None
for line_number, line in enumerate(text.splitlines(), 1):
    heading = heading_re.match(line)
    if heading:
        current = heading.group(1)
        catalog.append(current)
        records.append(f"{line_number}:{line}")
    elif line.startswith(dependency_prefix):
        if current is None:
            raise SystemExit("clause-without-heading")
        records.append(f"{line_number}:{line}")
        clauses.append((current, line[len(dependency_prefix):].strip()))

extraction = ("\n".join(records) + "\n").encode("utf-8")
if len(records) != 284 or len(extraction) != 36866 or digest_bytes(extraction) != EXTRACTION_ROOT:
    raise SystemExit("extraction-identity")
if len(catalog) != 142 or len(set(catalog)) != 142 or len(clauses) != 142:
    raise SystemExit("catalog-cardinality")

short_to_full = {}
for full in catalog:
    match = re.fullmatch(r"C1\.CC\.([A-Z]{3})\.(\d{3})@1", full)
    short_to_full[f"{match.group(1)}.{match.group(2)}"] = full

token_re = re.compile(r"([A-Z]{3})\.(\d{3})(?:\s*[–-]\s*(?:([A-Z]{3})\.)?(\d{3}))?")

def expand(segment):
    values = []
    unknown = []
    invalid = []
    for match in token_re.finditer(segment):
        prefix = match.group(1)
        start = int(match.group(2))
        end_text = match.group(4)
        if end_text is None:
            shorts = [f"{prefix}.{start:03d}"]
        else:
            end_prefix = match.group(3) or prefix
            end = int(end_text)
            if end_prefix != prefix or end < start:
                invalid.append(match.group(0))
                continue
            shorts = [f"{prefix}.{number:03d}" for number in range(start, end + 1)]
        for short in shorts:
            full = short_to_full.get(short)
            if full is None:
                unknown.append(short)
            else:
                values.append(full)
    return values, unknown, invalid

prerequisite_edges = []
target_edges = []
transition_edges = []
transition_declarations = []
unknowns = []
invalids = []
prerequisite_residuals = []
target_residuals = []

def residual_receipt(owner, segment, kind):
    residue = token_re.sub(" ", segment)
    residue = re.sub(r"[\s,/()]+", " ", residue).strip(" .;:-")
    residue = re.sub(r"\b(and|or|only|conditionally|the|exact|versioned)\b", " ", residue)
    residue = re.sub(r"\s+", " ", residue).strip()
    if residue:
        return f"{owner}|{kind}|{digest_bytes(residue.encode("utf-8"))}"
    return None

for owner, clause in clauses:
    if "depends on " in clause:
        segment = clause.split("depends on ", 1)[1].split(";", 1)[0]
        values, found_unknowns, found_invalids = expand(segment)
        prerequisite_edges.extend((value, owner) for value in values)
        unknowns.extend(f"{owner}|prerequisite|{value}" for value in found_unknowns)
        invalids.extend(f"{owner}|prerequisite|{value}" for value in found_invalids)
        receipt = residual_receipt(owner, segment, "prerequisite")
        if receipt:
            prerequisite_residuals.append(receipt)
    if "targets " in clause:
        segment = clause.split("targets ", 1)[1].split(";", 1)[0]
        values, found_unknowns, found_invalids = expand(segment)
        target_edges.extend((value, owner) for value in values)
        unknowns.extend(f"{owner}|target|{value}" for value in found_unknowns)
        invalids.extend(f"{owner}|target|{value}" for value in found_invalids)
        receipt = residual_receipt(owner, segment, "target")
        if receipt:
            target_residuals.append(receipt)
    for match in re.finditer(r"in state transition \u0060([^\u0060]+)\u0060, additionally consumes (.*?)(?= only\b)", clause):
        transition = match.group(1)
        segment = match.group(2)
        values, found_unknowns, found_invalids = expand(segment)
        transition_declarations.append(f"{owner}|{transition}")
        transition_edges.extend((value, owner, transition) for value in values)
        unknowns.extend(f"{owner}|transition|{value}" for value in found_unknowns)
        invalids.extend(f"{owner}|transition|{value}" for value in found_invalids)

edge_strings = [f"{source}->{target}" for source, target in prerequisite_edges]
unique_edge_strings = sorted(set(edge_strings))
duplicates = sorted(value for value in set(edge_strings) if edge_strings.count(value) > 1)
target_edge_strings = sorted(set(f"{source}->{target}" for source, target in target_edges))
transition_edge_strings = sorted(set(f"{source}->{target}|{transition}" for source, target, transition in transition_edges))

nodes = sorted(catalog)
adjacency = {node: set() for node in nodes}
reverse = {node: set() for node in nodes}
for edge in unique_edge_strings:
    source, target = edge.split("->", 1)
    adjacency[source].add(target)
    reverse[target].add(source)
self_loops = sorted(node for node in nodes if node in adjacency[node])
roots = sorted(node for node in nodes if not reverse[node])
leaves = sorted(node for node in nodes if not adjacency[node])
isolated = sorted(node for node in nodes if not reverse[node] and not adjacency[node])

seen = set()
order = []
def visit(node):
    if node in seen:
        return
    seen.add(node)
    for next_node in sorted(adjacency[node]):
        visit(next_node)
    order.append(node)
for node in nodes:
    visit(node)

seen = set()
components = []
def reverse_visit(node, component):
    if node in seen:
        return
    seen.add(node)
    component.append(node)
    for next_node in sorted(reverse[node]):
        reverse_visit(next_node, component)
for node in reversed(order):
    if node not in seen:
        component = []
        reverse_visit(node, component)
        if len(component) > 1:
            components.append(sorted(component))
components.sort()

clause_receipts = [f"{owner}|{digest_bytes(clause.encode("utf-8"))}" for owner, clause in clauses]
result = {
    "catalog": {"count": len(nodes), "root": digest_lines(nodes)},
    "clauses": {"count": len(clauses), "root": digest_lines(clause_receipts)},
    "duplicate_prerequisites": {"count": len(duplicates), "items": duplicates, "root": digest_lines(duplicates)},
    "extraction": {"bytes": len(extraction), "records": len(records), "root": digest_bytes(extraction)},
    "graph": {
        "edges": len(unique_edge_strings),
        "edge_root": digest_lines(unique_edge_strings),
        "isolated": isolated,
        "isolated_count": len(isolated),
        "isolated_root": digest_lines(isolated),
        "leaves": len(leaves),
        "leaf_root": digest_lines(leaves),
        "roots": len(roots),
        "root_root": digest_lines(roots),
        "scc_nontrivial": len(components),
        "scc_root": digest_lines(["|".join(component) for component in components]),
        "self_loops": len(self_loops),
        "self_loop_root": digest_lines(self_loops)
    },
    "invalid_expansions": {"count": len(invalids), "root": digest_lines(sorted(invalids))},
    "nonedge_residuals": {
        "prerequisite_count": len(prerequisite_residuals),
        "prerequisite_root": digest_lines(sorted(prerequisite_residuals)),
        "target_count": len(target_residuals),
        "target_root": digest_lines(sorted(target_residuals))
    },
    "plan_root": digest_bytes(raw),
    "targets": {"edges": len(target_edge_strings), "root": digest_lines(target_edge_strings)},
    "transitions": {
        "declarations": len(transition_declarations),
        "declaration_root": digest_lines(sorted(transition_declarations)),
        "edges": len(transition_edge_strings),
        "edge_root": digest_lines(transition_edge_strings)
    },
    "unknown_endpoints": {"count": len(unknowns), "root": digest_lines(sorted(unknowns))},
    "version": "r61-typed-graph-v1"
}
payload = (json.dumps(result, ensure_ascii=False, separators=(",", ":"), sort_keys=True) + "\n").encode("utf-8")
if len(payload) != OUTPUT_BYTES or digest_bytes(payload) != OUTPUT_ROOT:
    raise SystemExit("output-identity")
sys.stdout.buffer.write(payload)
'
```

Success is exit zero, empty stderr, and exactly one newline-terminated stdout line / 1,943 bytes / `c7af03c30f58e652a7554d8e7aab7ffb72de052889a5cfaa77ca99de5c3512f5`, exactly:

```text
{"catalog":{"count":142,"root":"16335a8dca09230b4b898f3c2944e16035a078ccf149047cf4c20293a344a8d8"},"clauses":{"count":142,"root":"4996a66d52481d3a45d8892831bad5a0f329124a1491adaa4f51b75f1eb54983"},"duplicate_prerequisites":{"count":1,"items":["C1.CC.SEC.008@1->C1.CC.REV.006@1"],"root":"c127ca6f4a0e9c075e69972ae361bc71b8a81f0c33c6a3b38343e229f39ec6ce"},"extraction":{"bytes":36866,"records":284,"root":"444895625ba399dc89708e23a88edc59dd78876fdb583e117f5a0aead8489fc3"},"graph":{"edge_root":"782913cdebb0ea4ff65c9a6d2f98bbb9dacf08748a91332b684dacdeaccffb11","edges":1571,"isolated":["C1.CC.IMM.022@1"],"isolated_count":1,"isolated_root":"40aa9983383592afc794538e0564f5119cfdcea65a84571a366c9ebeb6c978ee","leaf_root":"f9a187fba1f6f82d63796a87bb98b4c32565070094295a834663fa08cd385b07","leaves":9,"root_root":"ea40b75512f0e7c784088ca8d90b61ada3b2ac6119b937837e875fd95716ce97","roots":5,"scc_nontrivial":0,"scc_root":"e3b0c44298fc1c149afbf4c8996fb92427ae41e4649b934ca495991b7852b855","self_loop_root":"e3b0c44298fc1c149afbf4c8996fb92427ae41e4649b934ca495991b7852b855","self_loops":0},"invalid_expansions":{"count":0,"root":"e3b0c44298fc1c149afbf4c8996fb92427ae41e4649b934ca495991b7852b855"},"nonedge_residuals":{"prerequisite_count":48,"prerequisite_root":"36f2f2708b828618928eb750857e2a99ce913e0e7f38da48ade150d57f2f9ec1","target_count":2,"target_root":"d6e93b4252b0b889ceb012b83c4897db0df2f9b870d358f83f900493f53a7aa4"},"plan_root":"db0bbb1df5bb317d3bd2cdf313708fa8a10af8bd6da5a3635e96473a2465ed59","targets":{"edges":151,"root":"5e1c187e3f43f9e036f4a59bcb0b0873512988fc572b7041fe34f806dd960d44"},"transitions":{"declaration_root":"04734ce19af32acbdba86d236e2c38269f22be2c8b86c9942775570c54d258a4","declarations":7,"edge_root":"f86df867a21dfa537cbd4c4defdf9c390e51e919d4564c3655e84d719cb951c0","edges":10},"unknown_endpoints":{"count":0,"root":"e3b0c44298fc1c149afbf4c8996fb92427ae41e4649b934ca495991b7852b855"},"version":"r61-typed-graph-v1"}
```

The helper's mechanical whole-plan scan is admitted solely to verify the plan/extraction identities and derive content-silent typed ledgers. It emits no source line or contract body and grants no semantic coverage. A runtime/input/stderr/stdout/identity mismatch, exception, or need to edit/retry is `NON-EVIDENCE-INTEGRITY-FAILURE`: retain prior successful source receipts as history, but file no packet and respond exactly `TERMINATED`.

### Sole dependency-soundness lens and graph adjudication

Use the closed 142-ID catalog as node universe. Canonical prerequisite edges orient `prerequisite -> dependent`. Keep assurance `targets`, guarded transition `consumes`, and non-contract/human/external residuals in separate typed ledgers. The helper reports structural signals, not findings or semantic truth.

### R63-C01 — Wall-time/cancellation DoS bound is cooperative-only at the third-party-dependency and host-adapter boundary in a synchronous, no-async core

- evidence: The core is declared synchronous and cooperatively cancellation-aware, delegating blocking work to host adapters. `C1.CC.BYT.001@1` Cancellation (plan line 760): "bounded synchronous reads observe the context or are isolated by a separately admitted host adapter that publishes only finished immutable ranges; core code never waits indefinitely and does not import an async runtime." `C1.CC.FLT.006@1` Cancellation (line 1306): "adapter checkpoints around bounded primitive calls/chunks; an uncooperative dependency is isolated or the profile refuses," with No-claim (line 1316): "memory-safe dependency selection does not prove freedom from hangs, panics, logic defects, or side channels." The enforcement floor is the honest No-claim at `C1.CC.FDN.006@1` (line 508): "cancellation cannot promise rollback after host-visible publication or instant termination of an uncooperative dependency." No contract obligates the range adapter or the Flate primitive to expose a bounded per-operation latency or preemptible-isolation guarantee; "isolated" and "publishes only finished immutable ranges" are stated as outcomes without a mechanism (worker abandonment, process isolation with kill, or an adapter-honored deadline).
- requirement: Rev 7 §6 P6 (source lines 946–948): "Parsing, decoding, rendering, font execution, transformations, and validation run under explicit limits and cancellation. Potentially unbounded work is a design error." Rev 7 §11.2 (lines 2163–2164): "Wall time | Operational latency control" and "CPU quanta | Fair scheduling and cancellation." Rev 7 §11.2.1 (lines 2170–2172): "A wall-clock deadline is an operational cancellation source whose firing depends on host load."
- consequence: Wall-time and CPU-quanta budgets are cooperative. A deadline cannot fire while the single synchronous thread is blocked inside an un-checkpointed adapter read or inside a looping generic Flate call on bounded-but-adversarial input. The plan's own no-claims concede this but do not convert the concession into a required boundary contract, so the operational-latency-DoS guarantee for the two non-project-owned execution surfaces (host source adapter; reviewed generic Flate primitive) rests on an unenforced assumption rather than an interface. This is the residual attacker-controlled latency surface that survives after every project-owned decoder is correctly bounded by input-unit checkpointing.
- repair boundary (never written here): at the FDN.006 / BYT.001 / FLT.006 boundary, add an explicit adapter/dependency contract requiring a bounded per-operation latency or preemptible-isolation guarantee (deadline-honoring read, isolated-worker abandonment, or documented refuse-if-unpreemptible) so wall-time cancellation is enforceable rather than cooperative-only at those two surfaces; retain the existing honest no-claims.
- loss guard: any repair must preserve the synchronous, no-ambient-async-runtime core law (BYT.001/BYT.002 and §4.1 Async-runtime guard, line 186), the "publishes only finished immutable ranges" immutability guarantee, source-change revocation (BYT.004), and the existing honest no-claims about imperfect erasure and dependency hang-freedom; it must not pull an async runtime into the core.

### Round verdict

Owner revision is not required. Under the sole security/DoS lens the plan states bounded, terminating, resource-accounted, and adversary-aware behavior with typed evidence, refusal, and cancellation interfaces across its declared inputs, state transitions, parsing/decoding, xref/revision graphs, filter chains (cumulative-expansion caps, transactional publication, poisoned-drop reconciliation), credentialed read decryption (bounded attempts and KDF/cryptographic-operation work, non-export keys, tenant/credential/revision partitioning, one-time container decryption), bounded recovery (multi-dimensional non-resettable search caps plus the retained-alternative whole-document-security law), metadata/XML (DTD/external-entity/XInclude refusal with depth/node/attribute/entity limits), caches (attacker-keyed DoS-resistant policy, no shared-cache amplification), diagnostics (reserved capacity against floods), the CLI attack surface, and the reachable-panic gate. The Rev 7 §11.2 limit-dimension set is completely accounted for (active vs. explicitly dormant for the excluded rendering/execution surfaces), and §10.3 rule 6 (no whole-document security claim from partial coverage) is upheld across strict, partial-source, and recovery-alternative paths. The single C is a judgment-level hardening at the uncooperative-dependency/host-adapter latency boundary; it does not establish a violated requirement, since the plan honestly no-claims it and the synchronous, no-async design is a legitimate choice. This lens is therefore marginal-only, with the sole residual uncertainty confined to that concession, which the owner may address without altering any semantic contract.

### R66-C01 — Aggregate per-operation memory budget is not enumerated among the active C1 resource dimensions

- evidence: FDN.005 enumerates the active C1 resource dimensions as "source bytes, objects/versions, reference depth, container depth, total decoded bytes, per-stream decoded bytes, expansion ratio, recovery branches, canonicalization/symmetry work, diagnostics, CPU quanta, wall deadline, and output bytes" (plan line 477), with no aggregate resident/committed-memory dimension. Memory is bounded only per-component (e.g., FDN.008 "protected memory, spool bytes" line 537; FDN.014 "memory" line 657; FLT.002 "private spool" line 1225) and via the `FallibleAllocationLease` (plan line 480). The plan's own residual is stated at line 488: pre-admission bounding "does not make every dependency allocation fallible or make process abort impossible."
- requirement: Rev 7 §11.1 names a first-class "memory budget" among the counters that "Recursion, retry, worker replacement, and parallel fan-out ... cannot multiply" (authority line 2123), and §11.4 requires large allocations be "explicitly reserved through budget-aware helpers" and "bounded before construction" (authority lines 2196–2200).
- consequence: because peak concurrent resident memory is bounded only as the implicit sum of several independent byte-caps rather than by one named non-multipliable ceiling, an input that stays under each per-component cap yet holds several large buffers live at once (e.g., source page cache + committed decoded container + output projection + canonicalization scratch) can reach a high memory high-water mark whose only backstop is fallible allocation — which the plan itself declines to guarantee against abort. That is a graceful-degradation / DoS-surface gap relative to the kernel's explicit memory-budget counter.
- repair boundary: smallest adequate repair is to add one explicit active C1 "peak resident/committed memory" dimension to FDN.005 (charged and reserved like the other non-resettable counters), or, if the intended aggregate bound is the sequenced/summed per-component byte-caps, to state that aggregation rule explicitly at FDN.005 and cite §11.1's memory budget. No repair is written here.
- loss guard: preserve the existing per-component byte-caps, the `FallibleAllocationLease` reserve-then-allocate coupling, the "reserves worst-case concurrent private work before launch" rule (line 480), and the honest no-abort no-claim (line 488); any addition must not weaken graceful degradation into a hard-abort promise.

### Round verdict

Owner revision is not required under the security/DoS lens. The plan's DoS and resource-safety architecture is thorough and consistent with the controlling authority: every Rev 7 §11.2 limit dimension is accounted for as an active or explicitly dormant C1 dimension (FDN.005 line 477), and the classic attacker surfaces each have cited local mechanisms — decompression bombs (expansion-ratio / per-stream / total-decoded caps in FLT.005/FLT.006/FLT.008), attacker-keyed hash flooding (FDN.013, closing audit row R2-N2), unbounded recursion/cycles (SYN.004 / DOC.001 / REV.003 depth-plus-cycle caps), XML entity / XXE expansion (DOC.015 disables DTDs and general/parameter entities and XInclude, line 1871), external-file fetch / SSRF (FLT.001 admits no external fetch by default; FDN.007 grants no network capability), double-decryption amplification (SEC.008 / REV.006 at-most-once container decryption), combinatorial recovery (REC.006 multi-dimensional caps, line 2003), mmap-truncation faults (BYT.001 refuses mutable/truncatable mappings), reachable-panic DoS (FUZ.011 gate), and diagnostic floods (FDN.009 reserved capacity plus `diagnostics_truncated`). The single surviving observation (C01) is a completeness/explicitness gap about naming an aggregate memory budget, not a violated requirement; it is marginal and does not block later plan-to-execution conversion. Remaining uncertainty: whether the implementation's per-component caps plus fallible allocation are intended to constitute the §11.1 memory budget — an owner question, not a confirmed defect. This lens is marginal-only.
- termination state: TERMINATED

### R12-A01 — Standalone-implementability headline outruns the slice

- evidence: `plans/CYCLE_1_DELTA_PLAN.md` §9.6, lines 3,359–3,388 says the slice can be implemented “without reading another section,” but step 8 delegates to undefined-in-slice `REC.010 consequence checks`. The mandatory check/result surface—including all seven typed materiality axes, cost attribution, availability, and the gate for minting `RecoveryHypothesisId`—is specified only outside the slice at §8.8, lines 2,065–2,082 (and summarized before the slice at lines 3,353–3,357). The standalone claim therefore omits an implementation-bearing obligation that its own steps require.
- requirement: `OVERNIGHT_GOAL.md` G3 (line 66) requires self-contained prose from which a fresh agent can implement without asking, with dependencies explicit and non-obvious choices justified; the sealed R12 lens expressly requires inflated or unsupported headlines and prose obscuring an implementation-relevant obligation to be reported. `AGENTS.md` Rule 20 forbids generated prose from strengthening its source evidence.
- consequence: an agent given only the promised slice can implement step 8 without the closed materiality schema, cost reconciliation, or finalization preconditions while believing the excerpt is sufficient; that can silently admit an under-specified recovered hypothesis.
- repair boundary: either qualify the opening sentence as an orientation/review slice that requires the named contract definitions, or inline the minimum exact schemas and gates for every delegated contract needed to implement the steps; do not add PDF semantics or strengthen the outcome.
- loss guard: preserve strict-before-recovery, the basis/view/final-hypothesis separation, all live alternatives and unavailable axes, credential secrecy, one-time container decryption, transaction-state distinctions, pending-card boundaries, and every explicit no-claim in lines 3,365–3,390.

### R12-B02 — “Those seventeen presentation fields” has no reproducible referent

- evidence: `plans/CYCLE_1_DELTA_PLAN.md` §5, lines 193–209 introduces the contract fields as fifteen bullets, while line 211 calls the antecedent “those seventeen presentation fields.” The first concrete contract at lines 391–409 uses a seventeen-heading template that separately includes `Card slots` and `Rationale`; §5 instead folds card state into the precondition bullet and does not name rationale. The intended counting/mapping convention is therefore not stated.
- requirement: `OVERNIGHT_GOAL.md` §1 item 11 requires a line-by-line de-slopification pass, and G3 (line 66) requires self-contained implementation guidance; the sealed R12 lens expressly covers vague referents and prose that obscures an implementation-relevant obligation.
- consequence: an IMM.002 registry implementer cannot tell whether “seventeen” denotes template headings, semantic subfields inside the fifteen bullets, or a different canonical record, so `ConsequenceAxisRecord` source-field links can omit or misclassify fields while still appearing to follow §5.
- repair boundary: name the canonical heading keys and their exact mapping to `ConsequenceAxisRecord`, or remove the unexplained number and refer to the fields by stable names; retain exact typed values and source-field links.
- loss guard: preserve every existing contract heading, card-state visibility, rationale where required, the extra consequence axes in line 211, `NotApplicable(reason_code, authority)`, and the ban on inferred defaults.

### Round verdict

Owner revision is required before this lens converges. R12-A01 is a confirmed unsupported standalone-implementation claim with a concrete missing obligation; R12-B02 remains probable because the intended seventeen-field convention may be the recurring template, but the text does not say so. The reserved vocabulary is otherwise scoped rather than promotional: the formal `Complete` outcome is bounded at lines 61–63 and 2,851–2,869, and the formal `best_outcomes` aggregation field is expressly narrowed at lines 3,553–3,555. ### R31-A01 — Strict-only OpenReport root contradicts the candidate kernel report grammar

- evidence: plans/CYCLE_1_DELTA_PLAN.md lines 2,089-2,106 define the base OpenReport semantic body and expressly exclude every recovery hypothesis, candidate view, and recovery-derived finding from its inputs and root. Lines 2,209-2,227 instead place later recovery status and candidate-specific findings in a separately rooted RPT.007 enrichment envelope while keeping the base report bytes unchanged.
- requirement: proposed Constitution v8 lines 1,020-1,039 make OpenReport a required report family whose semantic body records recovery status and the security/behavior reachability-and-effect inventory across every admitted revision, view, and hypothesis, with explicit analyzed or unavailable coverage. Lines 1,285-1,296 make each report family a closed semantic-body grammar, require every mandatory field for identity, and require a new protocol version for a material grammar change. Constitution lines 20-35 preserve the extraction's proposed, not-yet-ratified status; that uncertainty does not erase the local contradiction inside the proposed v8 adoption target.
- consequence: the plan and candidate kernel assign incompatible value grammars to the same OpenReport family. A separately rooted enrichment may preserve useful strict/recovery separation, but it cannot silently remove a mandatory OpenReport field or change which values participate in the report root while retaining initial-kernel-adoption status. Report, package, checker, and claim roots would otherwise disagree about identity.
- repair boundary: either make the C1 report projection instantiate the candidate OpenReport grammar, including typed unavailable coverage for deferred C1 semantics, or route the strict-root/enrichment split as an explicit kernel report-protocol change with its full change-control package; do not repair implementation here.
- loss guard: preserve immutable strict findings, the prohibition on recovery rewriting strict evidence, typed unavailability for deferred reachability/effect semantics, exact alternative retention, and the semantic-report/run-observation separation.

### R31-A02 — ValidationReport omits mandatory derivation and checker identities

- evidence: plans/CYCLE_1_DELTA_PLAN.md lines 2,269-2,287 define RPT.010. Its inputs and output body bind view, graph, profile, rule-set, per-rule outcomes, coverage, and producer/build provenance, but the body never binds an exact validation DerivationId or the checker build identity and checker protocol/schema version as mandatory report fields.
- requirement: proposed Constitution v8 lines 1,093-1,105 require ValidationReport to record the exact DocumentViewId, source-backed RevisionGraphId, validation DerivationId, checker build identity, checker protocol/schema version, profile, rule set, per-rule implementation identities, outcomes, coverage, locations, comparisons, and unsupported classes. Lines 1,287-1,296 require every mandatory family field inside the closed semantic-body grammar; a missing mandatory identity refuses report identity rather than becoming an optional provenance detail.
- consequence: RPT.010 can mint its stated ValidationReportId without enough proper-domain identity to distinguish a changed validation derivation or checker protocol. That is an omission from the candidate kernel report identity, not an ordinary implementation choice, even though IMM.004 later proposes a missing-identity check.
- repair boundary: bind the exact validation derivation and checker build/protocol/schema identities through RPT.010 inputs, semantic output, generated schema, checker, migrations, and gate consumers, or declare and govern a report-grammar change; do not supply source implementation.
- loss guard: preserve the three non-transitive C1 validation layers, explicit NotEvaluated/Unsupported/Indeterminate states, exact rule coverage, card gating, and host-observation separation.

### R31-A03 — Local G0-G6 restatement severs the versioned Gauntlet-tier dependency

- evidence: plans/CYCLE_1_DELTA_PLAN.md lines 3,528-3,539 define the C1 Gauntlet gate by locally restating G0, G1, G2, a G3 subset, G4, and a G5/G6 subset. That gate contains no G7 row and no binding to the versioned shell §27 taxonomy, §27.1 falsifier pairing, or §27.4 release matrix, despite the plan's own consequence-contract grammar at lines 193-209 requiring a Gauntlet tier.
- requirement: Charter lines 140-143 and Work Order lines 71-74 make the assigned Gauntlet tier the definition of done for every capability contract. Proposed Constitution v8 line 2,009 makes that assignment a versioned kernel dependency on shell/MONKEYBEE_SHELL_CORPUS_v8.md §27 and binds the entire G0-G7 taxonomy, G6.1 lanes, every §27.1 falsifier family, and §27.4 release matrix. Constitution lines 1,316-1,329 require Gauntlet obligations to be referenced or generated from consequence contracts rather than copied into a new local ontology.
- consequence: the current gate can drift from or narrow the versioned definition of done while still calling itself initial-kernel adoption. The exact shell §27 body was intentionally outside this review boundary, so this finding does not assign particular C1 contracts to particular tiers; it establishes the broken versioned dependency and partial local replacement visible in the admitted text.
- repair boundary: bind each contract and gate row to the exact versioned shell taxonomy, falsifier family, and release-matrix assignment, or route any intended taxonomy/pairing change through kernel change control.
- loss guard: preserve C1's no-external-action boundary, typed G3-subset unavailability, product-versus-campaign degradation, oracle-lineage disclosure, and all locally enumerated hostile-input lanes while restoring the governing versioned dependency.

### R31-A04 — Detected kernel changes have no activated change-control package

- evidence: plans/CYCLE_1_DELTA_PLAN.md line 33 declares kernel-touch: initial-kernel-adoption; lines 48-52 say C1 adopts and does not amend the kernel; lines 3,616-3,622 repeat that no listed kernel law may change and describe version bump, migration, claim-lapse review, and Charter change control only as a future response if implementation later reveals a defect. Charter lines 138-145 repeat the kernel-touch declaration and blocking cycle protocol; Constitution lines 1,982-1,992 additionally require a decision record, cross-domain impact analysis, migration/invalidation policy, updated no-claim boundaries, and explicit refreeze for structural change.
- consequence: A01-A03 would retain the v8/at-1 identity while changing report roots and the assurance definition of done. The hypothetical stop rule at line 3,620 is not an activated version, affected-root migration, claim-lapse disposition, Charter entry, or refreeze record.
- repair boundary: after resolving whether A01-A03 should conform or remain changes, attach one explicit change-control package to every retained touch and enumerate affected report, package, checker, receipt, capability, claim, and gate identities; if all provisions are brought back into conformance, retain initial-kernel-adoption and record no touch.
- loss guard: do not silently ratify the proposed Constitution, rewrite historical roots, backfill evidence, strengthen claims, or discard the plan's explicit stop-on-kernel-defect rule.
